mnemonics: H-CAP for Water Quality Indicators: Heavy metals, Coliforms, Acidity, Pesticides
F.A.S.T. for Water Availability Factors: Freshwater supply, Annual rainfall, Seasonal variation, Technological access
keyPoints: Only 2.5% of Earth's water is freshwater, with less than 1% readily accessible for human use.
Water quality is measured by physical (turbidity, temperature), chemical (pH, dissolved oxygen, contaminants), and biological (bacteria, viruses) parameters.
Common contaminants include pathogens, nutrients (nitrates/phosphates), heavy metals, and organic pollutants from agricultural, industrial, and domestic waste.
commonMistakes: Assuming all freshwater is readily available for use (most is locked in glaciers and ice caps).
Confusing turbidity (cloudiness) with toxicity (presence of harmful chemicals).
Overlooking groundwater as a major resource, leading to unsustainable extraction and aquifer depletion.
